**Bottom line for downstream:** VeriLearn's failure honesty is excellent *per instance* and nearly absent *under load* — there is no capacity model, no throughput budget for the human SME on the critical path, no control over re-verification cascades, no cross-consumer consistency bus, and no compute isolation between tenants. The three load-bearing fixes are a **verification scheduler with QoS/admission control**, the **SME queue and re-verification cascade turned into SLO'd, bounded, lineage-driven subsystems**, and a **canonical ledger + execution cache** that both relieves throughput and compounds into a moat. Ship those and the invariants stay correct-and-available as the product scales; skip them and VeriLearn is a system whose correctness guarantees survive scale only by becoming slower and slower until, precisely when it is most successful, it is honest but unusable.

---

## Completeness Review & Self-Assessment

*Final self-review of the now-complete VeriLearn PRD prior to sign-off. Scope reviewed: exec summary (`01`), the consolidated assumptions register (`02`), overview (`05`), four persona clusters (`10–13`), twenty domain specs (`20–39`), the prioritization/roadmap rollup (`40`), the requirements-traceability matrix (`45`), and seven critique lenses (`50–56`). Method: full read of every section; a persona-by-goal cross-check against the traceability matrix; mechanical verification of story counts, ID prefixes, and structural sections; workflow tracing across domain handoffs; and reconciliation of the connective artifacts (`01`/`02`/`40`/`45`) and security domain (`39`) against the domain corpus.*

**Corpus at a glance (verified counts):** 24 personas · 20 domain specs · **462 user stories** (203 MVP / 222 Should-Have / 27 Nice / 10 Future) · 7 dedicated critique lenses · **1 consolidated assumptions register** (`02`, ASM-01…28 + OD-1…8). Every domain closes with *Business rules & invariants*, *Cross-domain dependencies*, and *Key technical requirements*; every one of the 462 stories carries a priority, acceptance criteria, business rules, and an explicit *Failure & edge cases* block (story-count and failure-block-count match exactly in all 20 domains). Story-ID prefixes are one-per-domain and clean (`AUTH-`, `HOME-`, `VERIFY-`, `LEARN-`, `TASK-`, `TRUST-`, `REVIEW-`, `GAP-`, `TEST-`, `COMM-`, `EVENT-`, `NOTIF-`, `ANALYTICS-`, `SETTINGS-`, `BILL-`, `ORG-`, `ADMIN-`, `A11Y-`, `API-`, `SEC-`), and every cross-referenced ID resolves. **This is a genuinely thorough, and now internally consistent, PRD.** The two connective artifacts an earlier pass flagged as missing — a traceability matrix (`45`) and a prioritization rollup (`40`) — exist and are sound; the last remaining hygiene gap that pass flagged — a standalone assumptions register — **now also exists (`02`)**; and the exec summary (`01`) has been refreshed to the correct 462-story / 24-persona / traceability-and-prioritization-present reality. The residual weaknesses are no longer artifacts or contradictions: they are a well-documented backlog of *substantive* open questions that the critique lenses **name and own but the domain specs do not yet resolve**, four of which are Blocker-grade and constitute a required P0 pre-build workstream (Section 3).

---

### 1. Coverage checklist (the seven questions)

| # | Question | Verdict | Evidence / where it's thin |
|---|----------|---------|----------------------------|
| 1 | Every persona served by stories? | **PASS** | All **24** personas have a dedicated traceability table (`45`) mapping each goal → a real story ID → a named surface → a technical requirement, and every domain story is authored in `As a [persona]…` form. *Caveats, honestly flagged in `45`'s "Orphans" section and now booked as assumptions:* **GUARDIAN** and much of **DEVELOPER-API** are served largely by `Future`/prospective stories, so two roster personas have thin *buildable-today* surfaces. The lenses also identify **~8 integrity/quality roles that should be persona-owners but are not** (Section 3, G10) — the roster is complete for what it defines, with acknowledged, documented holes. |
| 2 | Every major workflow end-to-end? | **PASS** | The core loop (create → 6-stage pipeline → lecture+gates → rubric tasks → conflicts/sources/ledger → confidence-gated FSRS review → gap map → verified-only tests → certificate) is fully chained with explicit cross-domain handoffs and traced per-persona in `45`. The **Teams chain** (billing seat-ceiling → org provisioning → invite/SSO → shared-library landing → publish/assign gate → cohort reporting → SME certify workflow) and the **certificate lifecycle** (issue → public/programmatic verify → SME/T&S revoke → propagate) are both complete, and `40` sequences them into R1/R2/R3. *Thin:* no single consolidated visual **journey map** (`45` is a persona×goal matrix, not a swim-lane); the lenses surface *missing* workflows (winback, engineered first-session win, verdict-staleness lifecycle, SME triage/throughput queue) — documented in `51/53/56`, not yet in the domains. |
| 3 | Edge/failure/recovery present? | **PASS (standout strength)** | 100% of stories carry a *Failure & edge cases* block (mechanically verified). Every domain has dedicated failure/abuse/recovery stories (offline, idempotency, optimistic concurrency, gaming, consented support-recovery, honest degradation), and `39` supplies the security/incident half: breach workflow (SEC-17), source-hallucination guard (VERIFY-16), sandbox envelope + kill switch (SEC-18/ADMIN-06), prompt-injection defense (SEC-19). *Still thin (named in `56`):* no ledger **read-path** degraded mode, offline tolerance exists only for Review (not lecture/tests/conflicts), no LLM-provider failover, no re-verification-storm backpressure. |
| 4 | Traceability complete — orphans? | **PASS** | `45` exists, covers all 24 personas, and explicitly resolves orphans: **"Orphan features: none"** (every named surface traces to ≥1 story) and a candid list of **orphan *stories*** that deliberately point at future/not-yet-built features (guardian model, SCIM, broad API). Verified independently: sampled high-index IDs resolve; prefixes are consistent. *Residual gap:* the matrix traces surfaces↔stories but does not enumerate the prototype screens / Next.js app against story IDs, so *prototype-screen* orphans remain an asserted "none," not a demonstrated one (Section 3, G20). |
| 5 | A major feature AREA overlooked? | **PASS (with a large, documented open backlog)** | The areas an earlier review found *un-audited* are now covered: **security/data-protection** is a full domain (`39`, SEC-01…23) plus a gap lens (`54`); **learning-science** (`50`), **engagement/habit** (`51`), **trust/academic-integrity** (`52`), and **AI/Skeptic reliability** (`53`) join the business (`55`) and scale (`56`) lenses — seven lenses total. No major area is now un-examined. **However**, the lenses convert "overlooked" into "identified-but-unresolved": several are Blocker-tagged and remain open in the specs (self-signed certificates for non-Teams learners, no gold-set verifier eval/recall harness, indirect prompt-injection via retrieved sources, no cost governor on Pro-unlimited, unmodeled SME supply/capacity). **Genuinely still-absent area:** content **localization/i18n** beyond a language/timezone field (G18). |
| 6 | Assumptions explicit? | **PASS** *(newly closed — was the one outstanding gap)* | A consolidated register now exists: **`02-assumptions.md`** states **28 load-bearing assumptions (ASM-01…28)** across AI/Verification, Product/Learning-science, Business/Unit-economics, Ops/SME-supply, Security/Compliance, and Technical/Scale — each rated **Confidence × Impact** with a **cheap pre-roadmap validation experiment** — plus **8 Open Decisions (OD-1…8)** with recommended defaults. The premises that were previously implicit (SME availability/affordability, LLM-inference reliability and no-train terms, unit economics closing, the four signals' validity, tenant-crypto isolation, pricing) are now stated positively and testably. Section 2 is a pointer, not a re-listing. |
| 7 | Contradictions / overlaps? | **PASS** | Cross-surface overlaps (Active-Listening/FSRS settings, reset-gap-map, progress-visibility policy, cert revocation, guardian/minor handling) are deliberately disambiguated with "owned elsewhere" notes, and the epistemic firewall reads identically across all 20 domains. The self-consistency defect an earlier review found — a stale exec summary that undercounted stories and called the connective artifacts missing — **is now reconciled**: `01` cites the correct 462-story count and names `40`/`45` as present. What remain are **documented, deliberately-open tensions** (captured in the lenses and register, not latent contradictions): partial-verification reading vs. the hard close-gate (`56`); certificate validity snapshot-vs-live (`53`,`56`); Teams priced *below* Pro (`55`); "Transfer" measured on the taught set (`50 §1`); calibration-metric circularity (`50 §2`) — all tracked in Section 3, G14. |

**Score: 7 PASS (one a standout).** The functional core, the connective tissue (traceability + prioritization + assumptions register), the exec-summary reconciliation, and the self-critique breadth are all in place. The remaining work is not artifacts or contradictions — it is the substantive open-questions backlog, which is now *documented and owned* rather than *latent*.

---

### 2. Assumptions register — pointer, not re-listing

The spec's load-bearing premises are no longer implicit or reconstructed here: they live in **`02-assumptions.md`** as a first-class artifact. It contains:

- **28 assumptions, `ASM-01…28`**, grouped by category (AI/Verification, Product/Learning-science, Business/Unit-economics, Ops/SME-supply, Security/Compliance, Technical/Scale). Each row states the bet, a **Confidence** (our honest prior today), an **Impact if wrong** (Blocker / High / Medium), and the **cheapest experiment that buys the risk down before betting the roadmap on it**.
- **8 Open Decisions, `OD-1…8`**, each blocking or shaping multiple assumptions, with options and a recommended default (credential-independence ladder, cost governance under "unlimited," SME sourcing, verifier abstention + domain-risk gating, review-reminder default, model-update/re-verification policy, test-integrity/assurance tier, and the "Transfer"→"Application" relabel).

The register's own guidance is the link to Section 3: **every `Low-confidence / Blocker` row is a de-risking task that must precede or run concurrent with R1.** Those rows (ASM-01/02/03 verifier precision·recall·grounding; ASM-15/16 unit economics; ASM-17/20 credential independence; ASM-18/19 SME supply·throughput) are exactly the P0 pre-build workstream below. The P0 backlog is therefore not a discovery exercise — the problems are scoped and the experiments/decisions are already drafted in `02`; P0 is *executing* them.

---

### 3. Prioritized remaining-risk backlog — the P0 pre-build workstream

*The critique lenses (`50–56`) **surface** four Blocker-grade risks that the domain specs do **not yet resolve**. These are not hygiene items and not "found later" items: each falsifies a headline claim of the product's own thesis, each already has a scoped decision in `02`'s Open Decisions, and each must be closed before committing spend **beyond** the proven core loop. Severity is **P0** for all four by construction — they gate "complete" sign-off, not just a downstream tier.*

| # | P0 workstream item | Owner-type | Why it is P0 (which thesis claim it falsifies if unaddressed) | Register anchor |
|---|--------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|
| **P0-1** | **Credential independence / the self-signed certificate.** For Free/Pro self-directed learners (the majority of all cert-earners) there is no SME: the learner authors the topic, adjudicates their own Skeptic conflicts, attaches their own sources, passes an unproctored open-book test drawn from those claims, and names the credential — while the public verify page attests "verified & sourced" without disclosing the absence of independent review (`52 §1,7`; `53 §1`). | **Product / Research** (credential strategy) | The certificate is the product's flagship, monetizable, employer-facing output; the moment one employer or journalist notices they can mint themselves an "Advanced X, ≥75%, verified-only" cert on a topic they wrote and graded, the credential's market value **and** the honesty brand collapse in a single tweet. Decide the independence ladder (self-study → peer → SME → proctored) and disclose it. | ASM-17, ASM-20, ASM-24 → **OD-1** (+ OD-7) |
| **P0-2** | **Automated-verifier evaluation harness with measured precision *and* recall.** Skeptic accuracy is measured only as "SME agreement on flagged items" — precision on disputes it *did* raise. **Recall is invisible by construction:** a confidently-wrong claim shipped as `Verified` and never disputed leaves no trace, and model rollouts have no accuracy gate (`53 §2,3`; `50 §8`). | **AI-Evaluation / Research** (Model-Quality owner) | The verifier is the single instrument the entire product rests on; "an honesty-branded product cannot let its own verifier's accuracy be an article of faith." Endorsed error — a wrong claim wearing a green badge — is the one failure the product exists to prevent, and today it is uncountable. Stand up a versioned per-domain gold set as a hard rollout gate + canary, plus a continuous SME audit sample of *already-Verified* claims to make recall observable; publish both figures. | ASM-01, ASM-02, ASM-03, ASM-07 → **OD-4** (+ OD-6) |
| **P0-3** | **AI-cost governor for unit economics.** Pro "unlimited" runs a 6-stage LLM pipeline + Skeptic + execution sandbox (+ hard mode + effectively-unlimited revise-loop grading) with **no fair-use ceiling, no verification-credit metering, and no per-account budget**; the target personas (LEARNER-POWER, LEARNER-SKEPTIC) are structurally guaranteed-loss accounts, and per-user free-text topics amortize into **no shared verified-corpus asset** (`55`; `53 §10`; `56`). | **Product / Finance** | The business model "loses more money the better it does" — gross margin is uncapped-negative on exactly the users the product is built for, and the moat never compounds because every topic is paid in full, forever. Meter the expensive levers (hard mode, thorough re-verification) under a generous credit pool + overage, and pair it with a content-addressable canonical verified-ledger cache — the only path from O(users×topics) toward O(unique claims). | ASM-15, ASM-16, ASM-27, ASM-28 → **OD-2** |
| **P0-4** | **SME supply / queue capacity model.** The human SME sits on the critical path of *every* trust guarantee (publish/assign gate, source promotion, conflict resolution, quarantine release, injection containment, post-rollout re-review, cert-fraud review) yet is **unmodeled** for who they are, how they're sourced/paid/scaled, their per-hour throughput, triage priority, backlog SLA, and overflow behavior (`53 §9`; `55`; `56`). | **Product / Ops** | The availability of the honesty promise is bounded by human-review rate, and that bound is unmodeled — the realistic scale failure is "correct but unavailable." A backlogged SME queue silently blocks Teams publishing and cert issuance and shrinks the certifiable scope; unpriced staff SMEs destroy margin. Model the queue as an SLO'd, tenant-fair, prioritized subsystem with batch tooling **before** the Teams/B2B spend that exposes it. | ASM-18, ASM-19, ASM-20 → **OD-3** |

**One closely-linked fifth blocker, same owner.** Measurement-validity of the four honest signals — "Transfer" measured on the taught claim set (near-retention wearing a transfer label) and "Calibration" computed against a self-reported recall proxy (`50 §1–3`; ASM-10/11/12) — is the natural fifth P0, owned by the same Research/psychometric function as P0-2 and resolvable in part for free (rename "Transfer" → "Application" per **OD-8**). It rides alongside the four above; an honesty brand must not ship signals its own Skeptic would flag.

*The `50–56` lenses also carry a deep P1/P2 tail — indirect prompt injection via retrieved/promoted content (`53 §4`), quote-level grounding for `Verified·source` (`53 §5`), test-taker identity/proctoring and item security (`52 §2,3`), the ~8 unowned integrity/quality roles (G10), the engagement/habit half (`51`), data-residency/DPA/cookie-consent instruments (`54`), the ledger-change event bus and canonical store (`56`), and the guardian-oversight model (`54 §2`). These sequence into the relevant tier (certificates / Teams) and the build phase; they are not gating "complete" sign-off the way P0-1…4 are.*

---

### 4. Readiness verdict

**Verdict: The PRD is now build-ready to found engineering of the MVP core loop and its legal/security floor with eyes open, and — with the assumptions register (`02`) present and the exec summary (`01`) refreshed — the two hygiene gaps that blocked the prior pass are closed. What remains before a full "complete" sign-off is no longer artifacts or contradictions; it is the four-item P0 pre-build workstream (credential independence, verifier eval harness, cost governor, SME supply/queue model), which the lenses diagnose as Blockers and `02`'s Open Decisions already scope into concrete decisions and experiments.**

What is genuinely done and strong: the single-learner verification loop is specified to an unusually high standard (462 stories, each with acceptance criteria, invariants, cross-domain contracts, and failure/edge handling), the epistemic-firewall invariant reads consistently across all 20 domains, and the connective scaffolding is now complete and sound — **`45` traceability** proves every one of the 24 personas' goals lands on a real surface with a real technical obligation and declares (with a candid orphan list) that no surface is unanchored; **`40` prioritization** gives a defensible MVP decision rule, a reconciled 203/222/27/10 MoSCoW rollup, and an R1/R2/R3 sequence that correctly pulls COPPA age-gating, the encryption baseline, sub-processor DPAs, and the breach workflow **into R1** via real SEC-* stories; **`02` assumptions** states the 28 load-bearing premises and 8 open decisions the earlier pass found missing; and **`39` security + `50–56` lenses** close the audit-coverage holes so no major area is un-examined. A competent team can start the MVP loop from `20–39` today, sequenced by `40`, with `45` as the coverage spine and `02` as the risk ledger.

What still blocks calling it *complete*: the deepest substantive risks are **documented but unresolved** — a self-signed credential for the majority of learners (`52`/`53`), an unvalidated verifier with unmeasurable recall (`53`), unit economics that lose more money the better the product does (`55`), and an SME bottleneck with no supply or capacity model (`53`/`56`). These are Blocker-tagged analyses with scoped experiments, not closed items, and the domain specs have not yet been revised to incorporate their fixes or to add the ~8 unowned integrity/quality roles.

**Honest bottom line.** The thinness is no longer "whole areas are un-audited" and no longer "the connective tissue is missing" — both are fixed. It is that **the hardest, most existential questions the product's own honesty standard demands — does the certificate mean anything for a self-verified topic? is the verifier itself accurate? does the money and the human-SME throughput close? — are surfaced as owned, scoped, open blockers rather than answered.** Recommendation: **do not yet mark the PRD "complete."** Treat P0-1…4 (with signal-validity as the linked fifth) as a required pre-build workstream, executed against `02`'s Open Decisions, before committing spend beyond the core loop. The differentiator is real and now well-specified end to end, front to back; the credential-integrity, AI-reliability, and business/scale envelope around it is diagnosed, owned, and de-risking-planned — but not yet closed.
